mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 365369M as of 2006-01-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative SPX returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 3+ months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.29% as of 2006-01-01 approaching restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in payroll employment (PAYEMS declining month-over-month) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE increasing > 0.3pp in 3 months) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134564K and UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2006-01-01
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a leading indicator of earnings surprises.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth produces negative SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% quarter-over-quarter signals a contraction in residential investment demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market peak
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%) and high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) indicates the Fed is in active tightening mode, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.7 and FEDFUNDS at 4.29% as of 2006-01-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% sustained for 6+ months signals a tight labor market with wage pressure and full employment, which in early-cycle expansions drives positive equity returns through earnings growth, but in late-cycle regimes triggers Fed tightening fears producing negative returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2006-01-01 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, UNRATE < 5.0% produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 3% annualized rate combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 3% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11233.5B and RSAFS at 365369M as of 2006-01-01
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong demand momentum
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
